name: Common wheat (food medium)
basis: per 100 g whole grain, fresh/as-is weight (~13% moisture)

components.name: starch
components.source: Derived from USDA FDC 168890 (carbohydrate by difference 71.2 minus fiber 12.2 minus sugars 0.41) + Shewry 2009 (wheat grain starch ~60-70% of endosperm)
components.evidence: DOMINANT CARBON/ENERGY SOURCE. Amylose+amylopectin; requires amylase for microbial utilisation. Value ~58-64 g/100g depending on cultivar.

components.name: dietary fiber (arabinoxylan, cellulose, beta-glucan)
components.evidence: Cell-wall polysaccharides, mainly arabinoxylan; slowly/partly fermentable, needs specialised glycoside hydrolases.

components.name: fructans (fructo-oligosaccharides)
components.evidence: Prebiotic fructose oligomers (inulin/levan-type); fermentable by fructan-degrading microbes. Range 1-2 g/100g; midpoint reported.

components.name: sucrose
components.source: Baseline wheat-flour value 2.16 mg/g cited in Kovacevic 2025 (PMC12114655)
components.evidence: Main free disaccharide; readily fermentable.

components.name: maltose
components.evidence: Free disaccharide from starch turnover; readily fermentable.

components.name: D-fructose
components.evidence: Free monosaccharide; readily fermentable.

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence: Free monosaccharide; preferred fermentable sugar. Note total free sugars ~0.41 g/100g (USDA) consistent with sum of these four + raffinose.

components.name: raffinose
components.source: FooDB FOOD00187 + Huynh 2008; wheat grain raffinose ~0.2-0.7%
components.evidence: Trisaccharide (gal-glu-fru); fermentable by alpha-galactosidase-positive microbes. Midpoint of literature range.

components.name: malic acid
components.evidence: Confirmed present in all wheat cultivar grains (HPLC-DAD); 3rd most abundant organic acid after glutamic and tartaric. Trace level (mg/100g), not individually quantified in accessible text.

components.name: tartaric acid
components.evidence: 2nd most abundant grain organic acid; higher in Triticum turgidum than T. aestivum. Trace level.

components.name: glutamic acid (free, as organic acid)
components.evidence: Reported as the MAJOR organic acid by HPLC in wheat grain (also an amino acid). Free-pool fraction only; distinct from protein-bound glutamate below.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.evidence: Present; low oxalate/calcium ratio noted. Trace.

components.name: shikimic acid
components.evidence: Present (shikimate pathway intermediate). Trace.

components.name: fumaric acid
components.evidence: Present; TCA-cycle intermediate. Trace.

components.name: L-glutamate (protein-derived)
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid (gliadin/glutenin gluten proteins).

components.name: L-proline
components.evidence: Gluten-rich; 2nd most abundant.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: Correct chirality is L (replaces FooDB 'D-Serine' artifact).

components.name: L-lysine
components.evidence: First limiting amino acid in wheat.

components.name: L-cystine
components.evidence: Sulfur amino acid (also L-cysteine pool).

components.name: L-methionine
components.evidence: Sulfur amino acid.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Mostly gluten storage proteins (gliadins, glutenins) + albumins; requires proteolysis for microbial N.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Dominant fatty acid (~60% of total lipid).

components.name: palmitic acid (16:0)
components.evidence: Main saturated fatty acid.

components.name: oleic acid (18:1 n-9)
components.evidence: Main monounsaturated fatty acid.

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: SFA 0.27, MUFA 0.20, PUFA 0.63 g/100g. Low-fat matrix; includes octanoic acid trace.

components.name: phosphorus
components.evidence: Largely bound as phytate (phytic acid); low bioavailability without phytase.

components.name: iron
components.evidence: Non-heme Fe; part phytate-bound.

components.name: selenium
components.evidence: Notably Se-rich (soil-dependent).

components.name: sulfur / sulfate
components.evidence: Present (cysteine/methionine + inorganic S); genuine, not quantified here.

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: Trace electrolyte; kept.

components.name: molybdenum
components.evidence: Genuine ultratrace micronutrient; kept.

components.name: cobalt
components.evidence: Ultratrace; kept.

components.name: nickel
components.evidence: Ultratrace; kept.

components.name: boron
components.evidence: Genuine plant ultratrace element; kept (no BiGG met).

components.name: niacin (B3)
components.evidence: Much is bound (niacytin) with low bioavailability.

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.evidence: Present (~5-6 mcg/100g in literature); genuine, not in USDA panel.

components.name: vitamin E (alpha-tocopherol)
components.evidence: Plus gamma-tocopherol (germ oil).

components.name: choline
components.evidence: Genuine (~30 mg/100g literature); membrane/osmolyte precursor.

components.name: glycine betaine
components.evidence: Genuine wheat osmolyte (wheat is betaine-rich, ~60-90 mg/100g literature); kept.

components.name: ferulic acid
components.evidence: DOMINANT wheat phenolic (bran cell-wall bound, ~50-90 mg/100g); genuine.

components.name: 4-hydroxybenzoic acid
components.evidence: Genuine minor wheat phenolic; kept.

components.name: vanillic acid
components.evidence: Genuine minor wheat phenolic; kept.

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: Very low in wheat grain (endosperm carotenoids are mainly lutein/zeaxanthin); kept as trace only.

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: non-nutritive toxic trace contaminant (soil uptake), not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Cadmium
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Lead
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Mercury
remove.reason: toxic heavy-metal contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Ergosterol C28H44O
remove.reason: fungal membrane sterol, not native to wheat grain; indicates mold/FooDB contamination, biologically implausible as a grain nutrient

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: preformed vitamin A is animal-only; plants contain none (USDA retinol = 0). FooDB predicted/implausible in a cereal grain

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: wheat protein contains L-serine, not the D-enantiomer; D-Serine is a FooDB stereochemistry artifact (corrected to L-serine in components)

missing_added: starch (dominant carbon source, ~62 g/100g) - amount added
individual free sugars with amounts (sucrose, maltose, fructose, glucose)
organic acids (malic, tartaric, glutamic, oxalic, shikimic, fumaric) - Rodriguez 2012
fatty acid detail (linoleic 18:2 dominant, oleic, palmitic)
quantified minerals (K, P, Mg, Ca, Fe, Mn, Zn, Cu, Na, Se)
quantified vitamins (B1, B2, B3, B5, B6, B9, E, K)
keep_uncovered: phytic acid (phytate - major P/mineral chelator in bran, no BiGG met)
dehydrodiferulic acids (5-5', 8-4', 5-8', benzofuran) - genuine arabinoxylan cross-links
lignans: secoisolariciresinol, pinoresinol, lariciresinol, medioresinol, syringaresinol, matairesinol (genuine wheat lignans)
phenolic acids: caffeic, p-coumaric, o-coumaric, trans-sinapic, syringic, gentisic, isoferulic, isochlorogenic (genuine wheat phenolics)
pectic acid, albumin, boron (genuine components, no direct BiGG met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 168890 (Wheat, hard red winter) - full nutrient panel: sugars, protein, amino acids, fatty acids, minerals, vitamins
Kovacevic et al. 2025, PMC12114655 - baseline wheat-flour free-sugar concentrations (sucrose 2.16, maltose 0.57, fructose 0.53, glucose 0.45 mg/g)
Rodriguez et al. 2012, Int J Food Sci Technol, doi:10.1111/j.1365-2621.2011.02886.x - organic-acid profile (glutamic>tartaric>malic, +oxalic, shikimic, fumaric)
Huynh et al. 2008 doi:10.1016/j.jfca.2007.11.005 / Verspreet et al. 2012 PMID 22324634 - wheat grain fructan 1-2%
Shewry 2009 (J Cereal Sci) - wheat grain starch content ~60-70%
FooDB FOOD00187 - phenolics, betaine, choline, ultratrace minerals
notes: Whole wheat grain is a STARCH-dominated medium: starch (~62 g/100g) is by far the dominant carbon/energy source, requiring amylase for utilisation; free fermentable sugars total only ~0.4 g/100g (sucrose>maltose>fructose>glucose) plus ~1.5 g/100g prebiotic fructans and ~0.3 g/100g raffinose. Organic acids are present but only at trace (mg/100g) levels (glutamic>tartaric>malic per Rodriguez 2012) - NOTE lactic/acetic acid are NOT native to raw grain, they only form on fermentation. Nitrogen is largely locked in gluten protein (12.6 g/100g); phosphorus is largely phytate-bound. Removed 4 toxic heavy metals, ergosterol (fungal), retinol (animal-only), and corrected a D-serine stereochemistry artifact.
